document.title: Project & Dossier Extension Catalog — Heritage Gold → LAWIM_V2
document.description: Complete machine-readable catalog of all project/dossier domain extensions required for LAWIM_V2

extensions.current_limitation: V2 Project is single-threaded; no support for parallel operational dossiers under one project goal
extensions.proposed_structure: Add parent_project_id (UUID?) for hierarchical multi-dossier linking. Add is_multi_dossier (Boolean) flag. Add dossier_count (Int) for active dossier count. A single project goal may spawn multiple child dossiers, each with independent dossier_state, matching pipeline, and participants.
required_fields: Project.parent_project_id: UUID? (self-referential FK, null for root projects)
Project.project_goal: String (free-text description of user objective)
required_relations: Project.parent_project_id → Project (self-referential N:1 for dossier hierarchy)
Project → Project (1:N, parent to child dossiers)
required_permissions: USER: create child dossier under own project
AGENT: create child dossier under managed project

extensions.current_limitation: V2 Project has 5 statuses (draft, active, paused, completed, archived) — insufficient for structured operational tracking
extensions.proposed_structure: Add dossier_state enum with 14 states: creation, qualification, matching, presentation, wait_demandeur, wait_holder, mise_en_relation, visit, negotiation, agreement, transaction, closure, archive. Keep existing V2 status field for backward compatibility. Map V2 status → dossier_state in compatibility layer.
required_fields: Project.dossier_state: Enum (creation | qualification | matching | presentation | wait_demandeur | wait_holder | mise_en_relation | visit | negotiation | agreement | transaction | closure | archive)
Project.matching_status: Enum (not_started | in_progress | completed | rematching | failed)
required_states: Project.dossier_state ∈ {creation, qualification, matching, presentation, wait_demandeur, wait_holder, mise_en_relation, visit, negotiation, agreement, transaction, closure, archive}
Legacy mapping: draft→creation/qualification, active→matching/presentation/wait_*, paused→paused (retained), completed→closure, archived→archive
extensions.human_decision_question: State granularity: full 14-state model or simplified subset for MVP

extensions.current_limitation: No dossier_type classification — project_type serves double duty as journey and operational classifier
extensions.proposed_structure: Add dossier_type enum: purchase, rental, sale, investment, professional_search, service_request, financing, other. Separates operational classification from project_type (which remains as user-facing journey type). Enables per-type matching strategies, SLA thresholds, and document requirements.
required_fields: Project.dossier_type: Enum (purchase | rental | sale | investment | professional_search | service_request | financing | other)
required_states: Project.dossier_type ∈ {purchase, rental, sale, investment, professional_search, service_request, financing, other}
H0.5_matrices_impacted: All qualification matrices — dossier_type filters applicable matrix families

extensions.current_limitation: No free-text goal field; project intent is captured only by project_type enum
extensions.proposed_structure: Add project_goal (Text) field for free-text user objective description. Auto-populated from intent source_text when available. Editable by user. Provides context for agents and matching engine.

extensions.current_limitation: No consent mechanism exists in V2 — contact is revealed unilaterally
extensions.proposed_structure: Add double_consent_status enum to Project: not_started, demandeur_interested, holder_contacted, holder_favorable, consent_obtained, refused, expired. Create Consent entity with consent_id, project_id, demandeur_id, holder_id, property_id, demandeur_consented_at, holder_consented_at, consent_obtained_at, expires_at. Both parties must explicitly consent before contact is revealed. Implement 72h expiry with reminder chain.
required_fields: Project.double_consent_status: Enum (not_started | demandeur_interested | holder_contacted | holder_favorable | consent_obtained | refused | expired)
required_states: Project.double_consent_status ∈ {not_started, demandeur_interested, holder_contacted, holder_favorable, consent_obtained, refused, expired}
extensions.human_decision_question: Double consent mandatory for all match types or configurable per dossier type

extensions.current_limitation: No consent expiry, renewal, or revocation workflow exists in V2
extensions.proposed_structure: Implement consent expiry (30 days after consent_obtained if no action). Add renewal workflow: expired consents trigger a new consent flow with the same or different property. Revocation: either party may revoke consent at any time before contact reveal. Replace: new consent supersedes old (e.g., different property).
required_fields: Consent.renewed_from_id: UUID? (self-referential, links to previous consent)
Consent.replaced_by_id: UUID? (self-referential, links to superseding consent)

extensions.current_limitation: No holder decision tracking exists in V2; holders cannot express conditional acceptance or refusal reasons
extensions.proposed_structure: Create HolderDecision entity to track the holder's response to a demandeur's interest. Support states: pending, favorable, refused, conditional, silence. Capture refusal reasons (price_disagreement, timeline_mismatch, already_negotiating, unavailable, other). Support conditional acceptance with condition parameters (price threshold, timeline). Implement 72h deadline with 4-tier silence escalation.
required_fields: HolderDecision.decision: Enum (pending | favorable | refused | conditional | silence)
HolderDecision.refusal_reason: Enum? (price_disagreement | timeline_mismatch | already_negotiating | unavailable | other)
extensions.human_decision_question: Holder silence escalation tier actions and timing (Gold: 24/48/72h tiers)

extensions.current_limitation: No silence escalation mechanism exists in V2
extensions.proposed_structure: Implement 4-tier silence escalation on HolderDecision. Tier 1 (24h): first reminder via preferred channel. Tier 2 (48h): second reminder + agent notification. Tier 3 (72h): final reminder + property flagged 'to_confirm'. Tier 4 (72h+): property deprioritized, rematching triggered, admin notified. Each tier logs escalation event and updates HolderDecision.escalation_tier.
required_fields: HolderDecision.escalation_history: JSON[] (array of {tier, action, timestamp, triggered_by})
Project.holder_silence_flag: Boolean (default: false, set to true at tier 3+)

extensions.current_limitation: V2 has no structured progressive qualification flow; qualification questions are ad-hoc
extensions.proposed_structure: Add qualification_step (Int 0-10) field tracking current position in 10-step qualification order. Add qualification_data (JSON) storing captured answers keyed by step. Steps: 1=Intention, 2=Type, 3=City, 4=Neighborhood, 5=Budget, 6=Timeline, 7=Criteria, 8=Preferences, 9=Confirmation, 10=Escalade. Steps 1,2,3,5,6,9 are required; 4,7,8,10 optional. Back-navigation permitted. Skip logic based on intent/type.
required_permissions: AGENT: fill qualification on behalf of user
SYSTEM: auto-skip steps based on skip logic
H0.5_matrices_impacted: All qualification matrices — qualification data feeds matrix field values

extensions.source_concept: Rematching engine with triggers and exclusions
extensions.current_limitation: No rematching logic exists in V2; once a match fails, there is no automatic recalculation or re-proposal
extensions.proposed_domain: Domain 5: Dossier/Project Model + Domain 6: Matching Engine
extensions.proposed_structure: Add rematching_count (Int) and max_rematches (Int, default=3) to Project. Implement 10 rematching triggers (demandeur refuses, holder refuses, consent expires, visit fails, negotiation fails, match expires, new property listed, dossier updated, holder silence, holder unavailable). Exclude refused/expired properties with configurable cooldown periods. Progressive search expansion per rematch cycle.
required_fields: Project.rematch_history: JSON[] (array of {trigger, excluded_property_id, new_property_id, timestamp, expansion_level})
required_relations: Match.rematch_count: Int (rematch count for this specific match pair)
required_permissions: SYSTEM: trigger rematch on matching events
required_states: Rematch exhaustion: rematching_count == max_rematches → escalate to agent
H0.5_matrices_impacted: Matching engine — rematch triggers recalculate match scores
extensions.human_decision_question: Rematch exclusion cooldown durations: Gold defaults or configurable per property family

extensions.current_limitation: No progressive expansion strategy exists in V2 matching
extensions.proposed_structure: Implement 4-level progressive search expansion: Initial (exact city+neighborhood, full criteria), Rematch 1 (same city, budget ±10%, surface ±15%), Rematch 2 (same region, budget ±20%, surface ±25%), Rematch 3 (any region, budget ±30%, relaxed criteria). Store expansion_level on Project. Each rematch trigger increments expansion level.
required_fields: Project.search_parameters: JSON (current active search parameters including expansion modifications)
H0.5_matrices_impacted: Geographic scoring — expansion level modifies distance thresholds

extensions.source_concept: Participant tracking with roles and lifecycle
extensions.current_limitation: V2 has no formal participant model; users are associated with projects implicitly via owner_id and agent fields
extensions.proposed_structure: Create ProjectParticipant entity with id, project_id, user_id, role (owner, demandeur, holder, agent, notaire, professional, assistant, observer), status (active, pending, removed, declined), invited_at, joined_at, removed_at, removal_reason, permissions (JSON[]), is_primary (Boolean), metadata (JSON). Support participant lifecycle: Invited → Pending → Active → Removed/Left/Declined. Track engagement per participant.
required_fields: ProjectParticipant.role: Enum (owner | demandeur | holder | agent | notaire | professional | assistant | observer)
required_states: ProjectParticipant.role ∈ {owner, demandeur, holder, agent, notaire, professional, assistant, observer}

extensions.current_limitation: No engagement tracking exists per participant in V2
extensions.proposed_structure: Add engagement tracking fields to ProjectParticipant. Track response_time_avg (seconds), response_rate (0-100), message_count, last_active_at, engagement_score (computed). These feed into dossier health score and NBA prioritization.
required_fields: ProjectParticipant.response_time_avg: Int? (seconds, average response time to requests)
ProjectParticipant.response_rate: Float? (0-100, percentage of requests responded to)

extensions.current_limitation: No formal active request tracking in V2; pending actions are implicit
extensions.proposed_structure: Create ActiveRequest entity to track the current pending action in the dossier workflow. Fields: id, project_id, request_type (qualification_answer, match_review, consent_demandeur, consent_holder, holder_decision, visit_confirmation, visit_reschedule, offer_response, document_upload, information_request, payment_confirmation, signature), requested_from_id, requested_by_id, status (pending, fulfilled, declined, expired, cancelled), deadline, reminder_count, last_reminder_at, response_data, responded_at, context. Each dossier has 0 or 1 active request.
required_fields: ActiveRequest.project_id: UUID (FK → Project, unique — one active request per dossier)

ActiveRequest.request_type: Enum (qualification_answer | match_review | consent_demandeur | consent_holder | holder_decision | visit_confirmation | visit_reschedule | offer_response | document_upload | information_request | payment_confirmation | signature)
ActiveRequest.status: Enum (pending | fulfilled | declined | expired | cancelled)
required_permissions: USER: respond to own active requests
SYSTEM: expire active requests on deadline
AGENT: create/cancel active requests for managed dossiers
required_states: ActiveRequest.request_type ∈ {qualification_answer, match_review, consent_demandeur, consent_holder, holder_decision, visit_confirmation, visit_reschedule, offer_response, document_upload, information_request, payment_confirmation, signature}

extensions.source_concept: Linked properties registry with match context
extensions.current_limitation: V2 has no structured N:M link between Project and Property with match context and per-property decisions
extensions.proposed_structure: Create ProjectPropertyLink entity to track N:M relationships between Project and Property with full match context. Fields: id, project_id, property_id, match_score, compatibility_level, link_type (matched, proposed, interested, visited, negotiated, transacted, rejected, archived), demandeur_decision, holder_decision, linked_at, unlinked_at, unlink_reason, rank. Provides per-property decision tracking within a dossier.
required_fields: ProjectPropertyLink.link_type: Enum (matched | proposed | interested | visited | negotiated | transacted | rejected | archived)
required_permissions: USER: update own decision on link
required_states: ProjectPropertyLink.link_type ∈ {matched, proposed, interested, visited, negotiated, transacted, rejected, archived}

extensions.current_limitation: No link between project and service orders exists in V2
extensions.proposed_domain: Domain 5: Dossier/Project Model + Domain 3: Service Model
extensions.proposed_structure: Create ProjectServiceLink entity to track service orders linked to a dossier. Fields: id, project_id, service_order_id, service_code, status (pending, active, fulfilled, cancelled), linked_at, fulfilled_at. Enables services purchased within a dossier context (boost, accompaniment, photography).
required_permissions: USER: link service to own project
AGENT: link service to managed project
SYSTEM: auto-link service when purchased in dossier context

extensions.current_limitation: No mechanism to link professionals to a project/dossier in V2
extensions.proposed_domain: Domain 5: Dossier/Project Model + Domain 3: Service Model
extensions.proposed_structure: Create ProjectProfessionalLink entity to track professional engagements within a dossier. Fields: id, project_id, professional_id (FK → User), profession, status (suggested, engaged, active, completed, cancelled), engagement_scope, linked_at, completed_at. Supports notaries, surveyors, architects, lawyers, and other professionals required for transactions.
required_fields: ProjectProfessionalLink.status: Enum (suggested | engaged | active | completed | cancelled)

extensions.current_limitation: No source channel or communication preference tracking on V2 Project
extensions.proposed_structure: Add source_channel (whatsapp, telegram, dashboard, api, referral, agent_assisted), channel_session_id, language (fr, en, pid), communication_preference (whatsapp, telegram, email, sms, dashboard) fields to Project. Enables channel-aware communication and routing decisions.
required_fields: Project.source_channel: Enum (whatsapp | telegram | dashboard | api | referral | agent_assisted)
Project.language: Enum (fr | en | pid, default: fr)
Project.communication_preference: Enum (whatsapp | telegram | email | sms | dashboard, default: whatsapp)
required_permissions: SYSTEM: set source_channel on project creation
required_states: Project.source_channel ∈ {whatsapp, telegram, dashboard, api, referral, agent_assisted}

extensions.current_limitation: No health score concept exists in V2; dossier health is not measured
extensions.proposed_structure: Add health_score (Int 0-100) and health_score_components (JSON) to Project. Compute from 5 weighted dimensions: Completeness (0.25), Recency (0.20), Responsiveness (0.20), Progress (0.20), Risk (0.15). Health status: 80-100=Healthy, 60-79=Attention, 40-59=Warning, 20-39=Critical, 0-19=Stalled. Recalculate on state transitions, participant actions, SLA breaches, and daily cron.
required_fields: Project.health_score_components: JSON ({"completeness": N, "recency": N, "responsiveness": N, "progress": N, "risk": N})
Project.health_status: Enum (healthy | attention | warning | critical | stalled)
required_events: project.health_score_updated (payload: {project_id, old_score, new_score, old_status, new_status, components})
required_permissions: AGENT: view health score for managed dossiers
ADMIN: view health score for all dossiers
required_states: Score ranges: healthy(80-100), attention(60-79), warning(40-59), critical(20-39), stalled(0-19)
H0.5_matrices_impacted: NBA engine — health status triggers NBA recalculation
Agent dashboard — health score displayed for prioritization

extensions.current_limitation: No per-dossier priority or state-level SLA tracking in V2
extensions.proposed_structure: Add priority (p0, p1, p2, p3) to Project for dossier-level priority. Add sla_state_deadline (DateTime), sla_breached (Boolean), sla_breach_count (Int). Implement per-state SLA thresholds: creation=24h, qualification=7d, matching=48h, presentation=7d, wait_demandeur=72h, wait_holder=72h, mise_en_relation=7d, visit=14d, negotiation=30d, agreement=7d, transaction=90d, closure=7d. SLA breach triggers NBA recalculation and health score penalty.
required_fields: Project.sla_state_deadline: DateTime? (computed on each state transition)
Project.sla_breach_history: JSON[] (array of {state, breached_at, escalated_to, resolution})
required_permissions: AGENT: view SLA status for managed dossiers
required_states: Per-state SLA max durations defined in thresholds
H0.5_matrices_impacted: SLA monitoring engine — dossier states add new SLA dimensions
NBA engine — SLA breach triggers NBA recalculation
extensions.human_decision_question: Per-state SLA threshold durations: Gold defaults or configurable per dossier type
